hub_subtitle: Business English para Gestão de Riscos e Finanças

lesson.menu_title: The Stress Test — What Happens If
lesson.menu_desc: Reading a stress-test memo and talking through risk scenarios, in the first conditional -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 4 -- The Stress Test: What Happens If
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sergio Takashi Tanaka | Lesson 4 -- The Stress Test

listenings.text: Hello, everyone. This is Marina from the risk committee, with a short note before the stress test. If the market falls sharply next week, do not wait for me. If liquidity drops below the trigger level, you will unwind the weakest positions first, and you will call the committee within the hour. If a partner bank shows stress, we will cut the exposure and add a hedge. Our desk is resilient, so if we act early, we will stay well inside the limit. Please read the contingency memo before Friday, and send me your worst-case numbers. Thank you.

Hi Sergio, it is Paulo from the credit desk. A quick note on the stress test. I have finished the worst-case scenario, and the numbers look tight but fine. If the downturn is short, the portfolio will hold, and we will not touch the buffer. But if the shock is deep, liquidity will fall fast, and we will need to unwind two positions. If that happens, I will flag the counterparty to you before I escalate. Can we agree on the trigger levels before the meeting? If we set them clearly, the plan will be much easier to run. Thanks.

extra_audio.text: First, the stress test models a sudden market shock. If the shock hits, volatility will rise and liquidity will fall. If liquidity falls below the trigger, the desk will unwind the weakest positions. If a partner bank shows stress, the team will cut the exposure and add a hedge. Finally, if the worst-case scenario arrives, Sergio will escalate to the committee and inform the regulator.

paras: This memo describes what the desk will do if the market turns sharply next quarter. In the base case, the portfolio is stable and resilient. The stress test looks at a harder question: what happens if a sudden shock hits the market?

If a shock hits, volatility will rise and liquidity will fall. If liquidity falls below the trigger level, the desk will unwind the weakest positions first, before any limit is at risk. If a partner bank shows stress, we will cut the exposure and add a hedge the same day.

The portfolio is resilient, but the memo is clear about the worst case. If a deep downturn arrives, we will move to the contingency plan: we will escalate to the committee, inform the regulator, and unwind step by step. The main finding of the stress test is simple. If we act early, we will protect the desk; if we wait, the loss will grow.
reading.source: Internal Risk Committee -- Stress-Test Memo (adapted for class)

gist.prompt: Read once for the main idea. What is the best title for this memo?
choices: The market has already crashed and the desk lost control
A plan for what the desk will do if a shock hits
A report of what happened on the desk last quarter

items: If a shock hits, liquidity will fall.
The memo says volatility will rise and liquidity will fall.
The desk will unwind its strongest positions first.
It will unwind the weakest positions first.
In the worst case, the team will escalate to the committee.
It will move to the contingency plan and escalate to the committee.
The memo says it is better to wait before acting.
It says if we act early, we will protect the desk; if we wait, the loss will grow.
